title.en: AMMUNITION FUZES (blasting cartridge initiators F42B3/10; chemical aspects C06C); ARMING OR SAFETY MEANS THEREFOR (filling fuzes F42B33/02; fitting or extracting primers in or from fuzes F42B33/04; containers for fuzes F42B39/30)
Impact fuzes, i.e. fuzes actuated only by ammunition impact
with firing-pin structurally combined with fuze
without firing-pin
operating at a predetermined distance from ground or target by means of a protruding member
Fuzes actuated by exposure to a liquid, e.g. seawater (F42C5/00 takes precedence; time fuzes F42C9/00)
Fuzes actuated by exposure to a predetermined ambient fluid pressure {(fluid-pressure-operated switches H01H35/24)}
barometric pressure
Fuzes actuated by application of a predetermined mechanical force, e.g. tension, torsion, pressure (by ammunition impact F42C1/00, by exposure to a predetermined ambient fluid pressure F42C5/00)
Contact fuzes, i.e. fuzes actuated by mechanical contact between a stationary ammunition, e.g. a land mine, and a moving target, e.g. a person (F42C7/12 takes precedence)
Percussion fuzes of the double-action type, i.e. fuzes cocked and fired in a single movement, e.g. by pulling an incorporated percussion pin or hammer (percussion caps F42C19/10)
Time fuzes; Combined time and percussion or pressure-actuated fuzes; Fuzes for timed self-destruction of ammunition
the timing being caused by mechanical means
the timing being caused by chemical action, e.g. of acids {(F42C9/14 takes precedence)}
the timing being caused by combustion {(F42C9/14 takes precedence)}
Double fuzes; Multiple fuzes

Electric fuzes ({in combination with other fuzes F42C9/14}; proximity fuzes F42C13/00; {safety or arming effected by electric means F42C15/40; electric contact parts for fuzes F42C19/06}; electric igniters F42C19/12, {F42B3/12 - F42B3/18; optical initiators F42B3/113})

{Electric circuits for fuzes characterised by the ammunition class or type (F42C11/02 - F42C11/06 take precedence; mechanical fuzes having electric igniters for hand grenades or marine warheads F42C14/025, F42C14/045)}
{Power generation in electric fuzes (F42C11/02, F42C11/04 and F42C15/295 take precedence)}
with piezo-crystal
with current induction
with time delay by electric circuitry
Proximity fuzes; Fuzes for remote detonation {(F42C9/148 takes precedence; constructional details F42C19/00; mounting of antennas F42B30/006)}
{operated by variations in electrostatic field}
{for non-guided, spinning, braked or gravity-driven weapons, e.g. parachute-braked sub-munitions}
operated by intensity of light or similar radiation
operated by radio waves
operated by sound waves
operated by variations in magnetic field
{Mechanical} fuzes characterised by the ammunition class or type (F42C1/00, {F42C7/00, F42C9/00, F42C11/001}, F42C13/00, F42C15/00 take precedence)
for hand grenades
for torpedoes, marine mines or depth charges (influenced marine mines F42B22/04)
for fall bombs
for land mines
Arming-means in fuzes; Safety means for preventing premature detonation of fuzes or charges
{Combination-type safety mechanisms, i.e. two or more safeties are moved in a predetermined sequence to each other}
wherein the firing pin is displaced out of the action line for safety (F42C15/40 takes precedence)
wherein a carrier for an element of the pyrotechnic or explosive train is moved (F42C15/40 takes precedence)
wherein a securing-pin or latch is removed to arm the fuze, e.g. removed from the firing-pin ({F42C9/041 and} F42C15/40 take precedence)
wherein the safety or arming action is effected by inertia means (F42C15/196, F42C15/20 take precedence)
operated by flow of fluent material, e.g. shot, fluids (F42C15/26 takes precedence)
operated by change of fluid pressure (F42C5/00, F42C15/29 take precedence)
wherein the safety or arming action is effected by a blocking-member in the pyrotechnic or explosive train between primer and main charge (F42C15/18, F42C15/40 take precedence)
wherein arming is effected by combustion or fusion of an element; {Arming methods using temperature gradients}(F42C15/31 takes precedence)
wherein arming is effected by chemical action (F42C3/00 takes precedence)
wherein the safety or arming action is effected electrically
Arrangements for disarming, or for rendering harmless, fuzes after arming, e.g. after launch
Fuze-setting apparatus
Fuze-setting keys
for electric fuzes
Details of fuzes (except F42C15/00)
Fuze bodies; Fuze housings
Protective caps

Electric contact parts specially adapted for use with electric fuzes {(switches operated by change of speed H01H35/06; switches operated by change of acceleration, e.g. shock or vibration, inertia switches H01H35/14; fluid-pressure-operated switches H01H35/24)}
Primers (initiators for blasting cartridges F42B3/10; ignition means for rocket engine plants F02K9/95); Detonators
Checking fuzes; Testing fuzes
Subject matter not provided for in other groups of this subclass
